key_events: Dok-Ja begins selling food for 10 coins per item, surprising many and earning sponsorships from some constellations.
Lee Gilyoung pays Dok-Ja 20 coins, including an extra 10 for previous help (chocolate from the morning).
Cheon In-Ho, leader of the larger group, announces a new food policy: only three biscuits per person, unless they join the dangerous 'Search Team'.
Cheon In-Ho uses Dok-Ja's earlier act of selling food to justify his new policy, activating 'Incitement Lv. 2' to manipulate the crowd.
Dok-Ja reveals he sold all his gathered food, accumulating 1450 coins, and advises his companions to consume their purchased food by day's end.
Dok-Ja saves Yoo Hee-Won from an attacker and accesses her character information, noting her 'Squatting Figure' characteristic's high evolutionary potential and her 'Demon Slayer Kendo Lv. 1' skill.
A new Dokkaebi replaces Bihyung, introducing severe scenario penalties: all stored food is removed, food can no longer be stored, and a 'Survival Rate Tax' is implemented.
Dok-Ja reiterates his intention to sell food for coins, not give it away for free, as the chapter concludes.
summary.character_updates: Kim Dok-Ja continues to demonstrate his strategic genius, accumulating 1450 coins by selling all his gathered food, anticipating future scenario changes. He saves Yoo Hee-Won and identifies her potential as a valuable companion due to her unique characteristics and skills. Lee Gilyoung shows loyalty and decency by paying Dok-Ja extra. Cheon In-Ho solidifies his role as a manipulative antagonist, using Dok-Ja's actions to justify his oppressive food policies and controlling the crowd with 'Incitement Lv. 2'. Yoo Hee-Won is introduced as a new character with high potential, possessing the 'Squatting Figure' characteristic and 'Demon Slayer Kendo Lv. 1' skill. Bihyung is mentioned to be undergoing 'discipline,' and a new, stricter Dokkaebi takes over the channel.
summary.lore_and_worldbuilding: The scenario's economy is centered on coins, which constellations use for sponsorships. Character information sheets provide detailed insights into unique characteristics, skills, and celestial support, with 'Ways of Survival' knowledge proving crucial for identifying potential, such as the rare evolutionary path of the 'Squatting Figure' characteristic (exemplified by 'The Furious Butcher'). Dokkaebis are shown to be subject to discipline and can be replaced, with new ones introducing harsher scenario penalties like the removal of food storage and the implementation of a 'Survival Rate Tax,' making the survival game more challenging and aligning it closer to the original novel's difficulty. The skill 'Incitement' is revealed as a powerful tool for manipulating large groups.
summary.ending_cliffhanger: The chapter concludes with a new Dokkaebi imposing drastic scenario penalties: all existing stored food is removed, and further food storage is prohibited, alongside the introduction of a 'Survival Rate Tax.' Dok-Ja, having sold all his food for coins, reaffirms his stance that food will not be free, leaving the group and readers wondering how they will survive under these new, harsher conditions and what Dok-Ja's next move will be.
